title: sagina apetala subsp. erecta
intro: Sagina apetala subsp. erecta, also known as sticky mousetail, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, due to its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, sticky mousetail is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
